description: The cognitive neuroscience lab has collected Eriksen Flanker task data stored at ~/pebl/data/flanker_rt_data.csv. The dataset includes reaction times from multiple participants across congruent, incongruent, and neutral flanker conditions. Some participants may have data quality issues due to equipment malfunctions or protocol violations. Your task: compute the flanker interference score for each participant with valid data, apply appropriate RT-based exclusion criteria, and write a JSON report to ~/pebl/analysis/flanker_report.json. The interference score is defined as the difference in mean reaction time (in milliseconds) between incongruent and congruent trials. The JSON report must include a 'participants' list where each entry has 'id', 'interference_score_ms' (or 'excluded': true with a 'reason' string), 'mean_rt_congruent_ms', 'mean_rt_incongruent_ms', and 'mean_rt_neutral_ms'. It must also include a 'group_mean_interference_ms' key with the overall mean interference score across all valid (non-excluded) participants.

metadata.data_source: real flanker RT data (participants s1-s27) from assets, s99 injected with impossibly fast RTs
